quick_facts.label: Purpose
quick_facts.value: COMEDK UGET is a common undergraduate entrance test used for merit determination and B.E./B.Tech seat selection at participating COMEDK engineering institutions in Karnataka.

quick_facts.label: Conducting body
quick_facts.value: The exam is conducted by COMEDK (Consortium of Medical, Engineering and Dental Colleges of Karnataka) for the Karnataka Unaided Private Engineering Colleges Association (KUPECA) member-institution admission process.

quick_facts.label: 2026 examination status
quick_facts.value: For the 2026–27 admission cycle, COMEDK UGET was held on 9 May 2026; rank/score cards were scheduled to go live on 29 May 2026. The 2026 entrance-exam application window is closed.

quick_facts.label: Eligibility baseline
quick_facts.value: For 2026 B.E./B.Tech admission, candidates needed a recognized 10+2/2nd PUC or equivalent qualification with Physics, Mathematics and English, plus Chemistry, Biotechnology, Biology, Computer Science or Electronics; the stated aggregate minimum in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics was 45% for General Merit and 40% for Karnataka-state SC/ST/OBC candidates.

quick_facts.label: Test pattern (2026)
quick_facts.value: The 2026 test was an English-medium computer-based test with 180 multiple-choice questions: 60 each in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. Each correct answer carried one mark and there was no negative marking.

quick_facts.label: Counselling route
quick_facts.value: COMEDK uses centralized online counselling. For 2026, eligible rank holders had to register, pay the stated non-refundable counselling registration fee, upload documents, obtain document approval, and then participate in choice filling and online allotment.

sections.heading: What COMEDK UGET is for
paragraphs: COMEDK UGET is the engineering entrance route for participating COMEDK institutions, rather than a general national engineering admission examination. For 2026–27, COMEDK stated that KUPECA—comprising more than 150 engineering colleges in Karnataka—had entrusted it with conducting the common test and centralized seat-selection process. The 2026 brochure says that the score is valid only for admissions in the 2026–27 academic year and for participating COMEDK institutions or other expressly permitted institutions.
bullets: The result is used to generate a merit/rank card for the COMEDK seat-selection process.
A COMEDK UGET rank is not, by itself, an offer of admission: candidates must meet course eligibility requirements and complete the applicable counselling steps.
The official site described the 2026 process as an examination followed by centralized online counselling.

sections.heading: Eligibility: how to assess it
paragraphs: For the 2026 B.E./B.Tech cycle, COMEDK permitted Indian citizens, OCI candidates and PIO candidates to sit the entrance test, subject to the stated documentation requirements. The qualifying examination was 2nd PUC/10+2 or an equivalent State- or Central-Government-recognized examination. The final two years of study had to include Physics, Mathematics, English, and one of Chemistry, Biotechnology, Biology, Computer Science or Electronics.

The stated 2026 academic threshold was a pass with at least 45% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics for General Merit candidates, or 40% for SC, ST and OBC candidates of Karnataka state; the relevant subjects also had to be passed individually. COMEDK additionally stated that subjects must be passed from one board, and that diploma candidates were not eligible because the process has no lateral-entry admissions.

Eligibility rules can be revised if AICTE or Visvesvaraya Technological University (VTU) requirements change. Applicants should therefore treat these as the 2026-cycle rules and confirm the future-cycle brochure before applying or registering for counselling.
bullets: Check both exam eligibility and the counselling/admission eligibility for the intended B.E./B.Tech seat.
Do not assume a reserved-category qualifying-mark concession applies outside Karnataka: COMEDK specified that the 40% threshold was for Karnataka-state SC/ST/OBC candidates.
A candidate scoring zero in the entrance test is not assigned a rank in the 2026 rules.

sections.heading: Exam format and ranking
paragraphs: In 2026, COMEDK UGET was conducted only as a computer-based test (CBT). It comprised 180 MCQs—60 each in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics—in English only, for 180 total marks. A correct response earned one mark, and a wrong response had no negative marking.

The 2026 syllabus was based on the CBSE syllabus, with approximately 33% attributed to Class 11 and 67% to Class 12. COMEDK noted that, where the exam ran in multiple sessions with different question sets, it would use normalization and announce rank using normalized percentile. Its published tie-break order considered percentile in Physics and Mathematics together, then Mathematics, then Chemistry, then age (older candidate ranked higher).
bullets: Practice for a screen-based MCQ environment: candidates could navigate between questions, mark questions for review, and revise answers during the test.
The 2026 rank was generated from entrance-test performance only; the rank card was the basis for entering seat-selection counselling.
The 9 May 2026 examination was also administered as one combined exam for candidates opting for both COMEDK UGET and Uni-GAUGE E, but COMEDK and Uni-GAUGE outcomes/counselling were distinct.

sections.heading: Application and counselling process
paragraphs: The 2026 entrance-test application period ran from 3 February 2026 until 23 March 2026 after extensions, and that application window is now closed. In the 2026 instructions, COMEDK required candidates to register on its website, use the resulting login credentials to complete the online application and payment, and download the Test Admission Ticket (TAT) from candidate login when made available. Future-cycle dates, fields, documents and fees should be checked only in the corresponding official notice and information brochure.

After results, the 2026 central counselling process was online. Rank holders who wished to participate had to register in applicant login, pay the stated ₹2,000 non-refundable counselling registration fee, upload readable supporting documents, and receive an approved document-verification status before choice filling. COMEDK stated that online counselling could run for four rounds, subject to the schedule of statutory authorities; dates and seat availability were explicitly subject to change.

As of the latest official 2026 page reviewed, Round 1 choice filling had been extended from 17 July 2026 at 3 PM to 24 July 2026 at 2 PM. This is time-sensitive, 2026-specific information rather than a recurring deadline.
bullets: Preserve the TAT and download the rank card from applicant login; COMEDK stated that it would not send hard copies by post or email.
For counselling uploads, COMEDK required clear scans; unclear or mobile-photographed documents could be rejected.
Check the official counselling page for current seat availability, course options, round dates, fee structure and refund conditions before making choices.

sections.heading: Preparation approach grounded in the official scheme
paragraphs: Build preparation around Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics at the published 11th- and 12th-standard CBSE-syllabus balance for the applicable cycle. For the 2026 paper, that meant approximately one-third Class 11 and two-thirds Class 12 content. Because all three subjects carried the same 60-question weight, a balanced plan is more defensible than neglecting one subject.

Use the official CBT mock test when it is made available for the relevant cycle. COMEDK said its mock test was intended to familiarize candidates with the computer-based environment; candidates should use it to rehearse navigation, reviewing answers and handling a 180-question English-medium MCQ paper. For 2026, mock-test availability was restricted to candidates with successful payment.

Since there was no negative marking in the 2026 scheme, candidates could plan their final review around attempting every question while still prioritizing accuracy and time management. This is a 2026-specific strategy: verify the marking scheme in the next information brochure before relying on it.
bullets: Convert the official syllabus into a chapter checklist and schedule mixed-subject practice sets.
Use timed CBT-style practice, then review incorrect and skipped questions by topic.
Read the current official brochure and TAT instructions before test day; the centre, session and reporting instructions are candidate-specific.

sections.heading: What happens after the test
paragraphs: COMEDK publishes results and makes the online rank card available in applicant login. Under the 2026 rules, rank/merit derives from entrance-test performance; candidates with a rank card who also meet the qualification requirements may register for centralized online counselling. The rank card is needed for participation, but admission depends on document verification, choices submitted, seat availability, eligibility and allotment outcomes.

For the 2026 counselling cycle, COMEDK described choice filling followed by online seat allotment and up to four rounds, subject to the statutory timetable. College/course availability and fee structures are variable: COMEDK stated that they would be notified before counselling and later published revised and final availability/fee notices. Applicants should evaluate the specific allotment-round documents rather than rely on a static fee or cut-off figure.

The 2026 score was valid only for the 2026–27 admission year. A future applicant should expect to take the examination again for a later admission cycle unless a future official notification says otherwise.
bullets: Download and retain the rank card; COMEDK does not dispatch it as a hard copy.
Track each counselling-round deadline, because a missed registration, document-verification or choice-filling action can end participation.
Treat previous-year cut-offs, seats and fees as historical context only, not as admission guarantees.
